title: Cisco IOS Routing & VLANs
description: Learn commands for setting up VLANs, naming them, configuring access and trunk ports, and configuring OSPF routing.

tasks.prompt: Create a virtual LAN with ID '10'.
tasks.explanation: The 'vlan [id]' command creates a VLAN or enters VLAN configuration mode.
Naming a VLAN helps network administrators identify its purpose.

tasks.prompt: Assign interface 'FastEthernet0/5' to VLAN 10 as an access port.
tasks.explanation: Forces the interface to carry traffic only for the specified VLAN.

tasks.prompt: Configure interface 'GigabitEthernet0/1' as a trunk port.
tasks.explanation: Trunk ports transport traffic for multiple VLANs over a single link between switches.

tasks.prompt: Enable OSPF routing with process ID '1'.
tasks.explanation: This command initializes the OSPF dynamic routing protocol process.

tasks.prompt: Configure network '192.168.1.0' with wildcard mask '0.0.0.255' in area '0'.
tasks.explanation: The OSPF 'network' command defines which local interfaces participate in the routing process.
